_note: Append this block to skills_config.json under 'analysis_skills', and add the new category + filter definitions shown at the bottom

data_intelligence.label: Data Intelligence
data_intelligence.description: Foundational data understanding — underlies all four analytical modes

data_discovery.description: Explore available schemas, tables, and data assets to understand what analysis is possible from your configured data sources
example_queries: What data do I have available for training analysis?
What tables exist in my Cornerstone data?
What metrics can I build from my current data sources?
Show me what's available in my CSOD dataset

triggers_automatically_when: user asks about data availability before choosing an analysis type
metrics_retrieval returns zero results (signals unknown schema landscape)

data_lineage.description: Trace where a metric or KPI comes from — from source tables through bronze, silver, and gold layers — to understand how numbers are calculated and why they change
example_queries: Where does the training completion rate metric come from?
Which source tables feed my compliance dashboard?
If I change the Cornerstone assignments table, what metrics are affected?
Show me the data flow from raw CSOD data to my KPIs
Why did this metric change — trace the data back to the source

embeds_into.gap_analysis: When a gap is found, offer lineage trace to determine if the gap is a data pipeline issue (missing joins, stale tables) vs a genuine business gap
embeds_into.anomaly_detection: When an anomaly is flagged, run a downstream impact_analysis to show which other metrics are affected and an upstream lineage trace to check if the anomaly originates in a source table
embeds_into.data_quality_analysis: Lineage trace determines which upstream tables are responsible for quality issues found in downstream metrics

data_quality_analysis.description: Assess the completeness, freshness, consistency, and accuracy of your training data before running analysis — surface issues that would invalidate results
example_queries: Can I trust my training completion data?
Are there data quality issues in my Cornerstone dataset?
How fresh is my compliance training data?
Check for duplicate or missing records in my training tables
Are there nulls or inconsistencies in the learner records?

triggers_automatically_when: benchmark_analysis is requested (quality issues invalidate comparisons)
cohort_analysis is requested (inconsistent segment fields skew group comparisons)
funnel_analysis is requested (missing stage records collapse funnel stages)
anomaly_detection returns high anomaly rate (may be a data quality signal, not a business signal)

quality_dimensions.completeness: Null rates, missing records, sparse columns
quality_dimensions.freshness: Last-updated timestamps vs expected refresh frequency
quality_dimensions.consistency: Cross-table referential integrity, duplicate primary keys, orphaned foreign keys
quality_dimensions.accuracy: Value range checks (e.g., completion_rate between 0 and 1), expected categorical distributions

data_planner.description: Generate a complete data engineering plan for a given analysis goal — which tables to ingest, what bronze-to-silver-to-gold transformations to apply, which dbt models to build, and what ingestion schedule to run
example_queries: Plan the data pipeline I need to run a cohort analysis on training completion
What dbt models do I need to build for compliance reporting?
Design a medallion architecture for my L&D analytics
What data engineering work is needed to support my priority metrics?
Build me a data plan for the metrics I want to track

triggers_as_next_step_after.crown_jewel_analysis: Once priority metrics are known, plan the data infrastructure to serve them
triggers_as_next_step_after.metrics_recommendations: After metrics are recommended, plan the bronze→silver→gold pipeline to compute them
triggers_as_next_step_after.skill_gap_analysis: After skill gaps are identified, plan the competency data pipeline (position requirements, assessment scores)
triggers_as_next_step_after.data_discovery: After discovering what data exists, plan what's missing and how to build it
triggers_as_next_step_after.data_quality_analysis: After quality issues are identified, plan the remediation pipeline (deduplication, backfill, schema fixes)

lineage_subject.label: Lineage Subject
lineage_subject.description: Name of metric, KPI, or table to trace

lineage_direction.label: Lineage Direction
lineage_direction.description: Upstream = trace back to sources. Downstream = trace forward to metrics impacted.

analysis_goal.label: Analysis Goal
analysis_goal.description: Describe the analysis you want to run so the planner can design the right pipeline
